messages.name: Attribute
messages.description: Attribute represents an arbitrary attribute key-value pair.

fields.name: key
fields.description: Key of the attribute (e.g., "region", "cpu_architecture", etc.).

fields.name: value
fields.description: Value of the attribute (e.g., "us-west", "x86_64", etc.).

messages.name: PlacementRequirements
messages.description:
PlacementRequirements represents the requirements for a provider placement on the network.
It is used to specify the characteristics and constraints of a provider that can be used to satisfy a deployment request.

fields.name: signed_by
fields.description: SignedBy holds the list of keys that tenants expect to have signatures from.

fields.name: attributes
fields.description: Attribute holds the list of attributes tenant expects from the provider.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: SignedBy
messages.description:
SignedBy represents validation accounts that tenant expects signatures for provider attributes.
AllOf has precedence i.e. if there is at least one entry AnyOf is ignored regardless to how many
entries there.

TODO: this behaviour to be discussed

fields.name: all_of
fields.description: AllOf indicates all keys in this list must have signed attributes.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: any_of
fields.description: AnyOf means that at least of of the keys from the list must have signed attributes.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: AttributesFilters
messages.description: AttributesFilters defines attribute filters that can be used to filter deployments.

fields.name: auditors
fields.description: Auditors contains a list of auditor account bech32 addresses.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: owners
fields.description: Owners contains a list of owner account bech32 addresses.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: AuditedAttributesStore
messages.description:
AuditedAttributesStore stores the audited attributes of the provider.
Attributes that have been audited are those that have been verified by an auditor.

fields.name: attributes
fields.description:
Attributes holds a list of key-value pairs of provider attributes.
Attributes are arbitrary values that a provider exposes.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: AuditedProvider
messages.description:
AuditedProvider stores owner, auditor and attributes details.
An AuditedProvider is a provider that has undergone a verification or auditing process to ensure that it meets certain standards or requirements by an auditor.
An auditor can be any valid account on-chain.
NOTE: There are certain teams providing auditing services, which should be accounted for when deploying.

fields.name: owner
fields.description:
Owner is the account bech32 address of the provider.
It is a string representing a valid account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

fields.name: auditor
fields.description:
Auditor is the account bech32 address of the auditor.
It is a string representing a valid account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

fields.name: attributes
fields.description:
Attributes holds a list of key-value pairs of provider attributes.
Attributes are arbitrary values that a provider exposes.
fields.label: repeated

messages.description: EventTrustedAuditorCreated defines an SDK message for when a trusted auditor is created.

services.name: Query

methods.name: AllProvidersAttributes
methods.description:
AllProvidersAttributes queries all providers.

ProviderAttributes queries all provider signed attributes.

methods.description:
ProviderAuditorAttributes queries provider signed attributes by specific auditor.

AuditorAttributes queries all providers signed by this auditor.

services.name: Msg
services.description: Msg defines the audit Msg service.

methods.name: SignProviderAttributes
methods.description: SignProviderAttributes defines a method that signs provider attributes.

methods.name: DeleteProviderAttributes
methods.description: DeleteProviderAttributes defines a method that deletes provider attributes.

messages.name: ResourceValue
messages.description: Unit stores cpu, memory and storage metrics.

messages.name: CPU
messages.description: CPU stores resource units and cpu config attributes.

fields.name: units
fields.description:
Units of the CPU, which represents the number of CPUs available.
This field is required and must be a non-negative integer.

fields.name: attributes
fields.description:
Attributes holds a list of key-value attributes that describe the GPU, such as its model, memory and interface.
This field is required and must be a list of `Attribute` messages.
fields.label: repeated

enums.name: Kind
enums.description: Kind describes how the endpoint is implemented when the lease is deployed.

values.name: SHARED_HTTP
values.description: Describes an endpoint that becomes a Kubernetes Ingress.

values.name: RANDOM_PORT
values.description: Describes an endpoint that becomes a Kubernetes NodePort.

values.name: LEASED_IP
values.description: Describes an endpoint that becomes a leased IP.

messages.name: Endpoint
messages.description: Endpoint describes a publicly accessible IP service.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: Kind describes how the endpoint is implemented when the lease is deployed.

fields.name: sequence_number
fields.description: SequenceNumber represents a sequence number for the Endpoint.

messages.name: GPU
messages.description: GPU stores resource units and gpu configuration attributes.

fields.name: units
fields.description:
The resource value of the GPU, which represents the number of GPUs available.
This field is required and must be a non-negative integer.

messages.name: Memory
messages.description: Memory stores resource quantity and memory attributes.

fields.name: quantity
fields.description:
Quantity of memory available, which represents the amount of memory in bytes.
This field is required and must be a non-negative integer.

fields.name: attributes
fields.description:
Attributes that describe the memory, such as its type and speed.
This field is required and must be a list of Attribute key-values.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: Storage
messages.description: Storage stores resource quantity and storage attributes.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Name holds an arbitrary name for the storage resource.

fields.name: quantity
fields.description:
Quantity of storage available, which represents the amount of memory in bytes.
This field is required and must be a non-negative integer.

fields.name: attributes
fields.description:
Attributes that describe the storage.
This field is required and must be a list of Attribute key-values.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: Resources
messages.description:
Resources describes all available resources types for deployment/node etc
if field is nil resource is not present in the given data-structure

fields.name: id
fields.description: Id is a unique identifier for the resources.

fields.name: cpu
fields.description:
CPU resources available, including the architecture, number of cores and other details.
This field is optional and can be empty if no CPU resources are available.

fields.name: memory
fields.description:
Memory resources available, including the quantity and attributes.
This field is optional and can be empty if no memory resources are available.

fields.name: storage
fields.description:
Storage resources available, including the quantity and attributes.
This field is optional and can be empty if no storage resources are available.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: gpu
fields.description:
GPU resources available, including the type, architecture and other details.
This field is optional and can be empty if no GPU resources are available.

fields.name: endpoints
fields.description: Endpoint resources available
fields.label: repeated

enums.name: State
enums.description: State is an enum which refers to state of the certificate.

values.name: invalid
values.description: Prefix should start with 0 in enum. So declaring dummy state.

values.name: valid
values.description: CertificateValid denotes state for deployment active.

values.name: revoked
values.description: CertificateRevoked denotes state for deployment closed.

messages.name: Certificate
messages.description:
Certificate stores state, certificate and it's public key.
The certificate is required for several transactions including deployment of a workload to verify the identity of the tenant and secure the deployment.

fields.name: state
fields.description:
State is the state of the certificate.
CertificateValid denotes state for deployment active.
CertificateRevoked denotes state for deployment closed.

fields.name: cert
fields.description: Cert holds the bytes of the certificate.

fields.name: pubkey
fields.description: PubKey holds the public key of the certificate.

messages.name: ID
messages.description: ID stores owner and sequence number.

fields.name: owner
fields.description:
Owner is the account address of the user who owns the certificate.
It is a string representing a valid account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

fields.name: serial
fields.description: Serial is a sequence number for the certificate.

messages.name: CertificateFilter
messages.description: CertificateFilter defines filters used to filter certificates.

fields.name: owner
fields.description:
Owner is the account address of the user who owns the certificate.
It is a string representing a valid account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

fields.name: serial
fields.description: Serial is a sequence number for the certificate.

fields.name: state
fields.description:
State is the state of the certificate.
CertificateValid denotes state for deployment active.
CertificateRevoked denotes state for deployment closed.

messages.name: GenesisCertificate
messages.description: GenesisCertificate defines certificate entry at genesis.

fields.name: owner
fields.description:
Owner is the account address of the user who owns the certificate.
It is a string representing a valid account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

fields.name: certificate
fields.description: Certificate holds the certificate.

messages.name: GenesisState
messages.description: GenesisState defines the basic genesis state used by cert module.

fields.name: certificates
fields.description: Certificates is a list of genesis certificates.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: MsgCreateCertificate
messages.description: MsgCreateCertificate defines an SDK message for creating certificate.

fields.name: owner
fields.description:
Owner is the account address of the user who owns the certificate.
It is a string representing a valid account address.

Example:

fields.name: cert
fields.description: Cert holds the bytes representing the certificate.

fields.name: pubkey
fields.description: PubKey holds the public key.

messages.name: MsgCreateCertificateResponse
messages.description: MsgCreateCertificateResponse defines the Msg/CreateCertificate response type.

messages.name: MsgRevokeCertificate

fields.name: id
fields.description: Id corresponds to the certificate ID which includes owner and sequence number.

messages.name: MsgRevokeCertificateResponse
messages.description: MsgRevokeCertificateResponse defines the Msg/RevokeCertificate response type.

messages.name: CertificateResponse
messages.description: CertificateResponse contains a single X509 certificate and its serial number.

fields.description: Certificate holds the certificate.

fields.name: serial

services.name: Query
services.description: Query defines the gRPC querier service for certificates.

methods.name: Certificates
methods.description: Certificates queries certificates on-chain.

services.name: Msg
services.description: Msg defines the provider Msg service.

methods.name: CreateCertificate
methods.description: CreateCertificate defines a method to create new certificate given proper inputs.

methods.name: RevokeCertificate
methods.description: RevokeCertificate defines a method to revoke the certificate.

messages.name: DepositAuthorization
messages.description:
DepositAuthorization allows the grantee to deposit up to spend_limit coins from
the granter's account for a deployment.

fields.name: spend_limit
fields.description:
SpendLimit is the amount the grantee is authorized to spend from the granter's account for
the purpose of deployment.

enums.name: State
enums.description: State is an enum which refers to state of deployment.

values.name: invalid
values.description: Prefix should start with 0 in enum. So declaring dummy state.

values.name: active
values.description: DeploymentActive denotes state for deployment active.

values.name: closed
values.description: DeploymentClosed denotes state for deployment closed.

messages.name: Deployment
messages.description: Deployment stores deploymentID, state and checksum details.

fields.name: id
fields.description: ID is the unique identifier of the deployment.

fields.name: state
fields.description:
State defines the sate of the deployment.
A deployment can be either active or inactive.

fields.name: hash
fields.description: Hash is an hashed representation of the deployment.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: CreatedAt indicates when the deployment was created as a block height value.

messages.name: DeploymentID
messages.description:
DeploymentID represents a unique identifier for a specific deployment on the network.
It is composed of two fields: an owner address and a sequence number (dseq).

fields.name: owner
fields.description:
Owner is the account bech32 address of the user who owns the deployment.
It is a string representing a valid bech32 account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

fields.name: dseq
fields.description:
Dseq (deployment sequence number) is a unique numeric identifier for the deployment.
It is used to differentiate deployments created by the same owner.

messages.name: GroupID
messages.description:
GroupID uniquely identifies a group within a deployment on the network.
A group represents a specific collection of resources or configurations
within a deployment.
It stores owner, deployment sequence number (dseq) and group sequence number (gseq).

fields.name: owner
fields.description:
Owner is the account address of the user who owns the group.
It is a string representing a valid account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

fields.name: dseq
fields.description:
Dseq (deployment sequence number) is a unique numeric identifier for the deployment.
It is used to differentiate deployments created by the same owner.

fields.name: gseq
fields.description:
Gseq (group sequence number) is a unique numeric identifier for the group.
It is used to differentiate groups created by the same owner in a deployment.

messages.name: EventDeploymentClosed
messages.description:
EventDeploymentClosed is triggered when deployment is closed on chain.
It contains all the information required to identify a deployment.

fields.name: id
fields.description: ID is the unique identifier of the deployment.

messages.name: EventDeploymentCreated
messages.description:
EventDeploymentCreated event is triggered when deployment is created on chain.
It contains all the information required to identify a deployment.

fields.name: id
fields.description: ID is the unique identifier of the deployment.

fields.name: hash
fields.description: Hash is an hashed representation of the deployment.

messages.name: EventDeploymentUpdated
messages.description:
EventDeploymentUpdated is triggered when deployment is updated on chain.
It contains all the information required to identify a deployment.

fields.name: id
fields.description: ID is the unique identifier of the deployment.

fields.name: hash
fields.description: Hash is an hashed representation of the deployment.

messages.name: EventGroupClosed
messages.description:
EventGroupClosed is triggered when deployment group is closed.
It contains all the information required to identify a group.

fields.name: id
fields.description: ID is the unique identifier of the group.

messages.name: EventGroupPaused
messages.description:
EventGroupPaused is triggered when deployment group is paused.
It contains all the information required to identify a group.

fields.name: id
fields.description: ID is the unique identifier of the group.

messages.name: EventGroupStarted
messages.description:
EventGroupStarted is triggered when deployment group is started.
It contains all the information required to identify a group.

fields.name: id
fields.description: ID is the unique identifier of the group.

messages.name: MsgDepositDeployment
messages.description:
MsgDepositDeployment represents a message to deposit funds into an existing deployment
on the blockchain. This is part of the interaction mechanism for managing
deployment-related resources.

fields.name: id
fields.description: ID is the unique identifier of the deployment.

fields.name: amount
fields.description:
Amount defines the funds to deposit into the deployment.
It is specified as a coin amount (denomination and value).

fields.name: depositor
fields.description:
Depositor is the address of the account depositing funds into the deployment.
This must be a valid blockchain account address.

messages.name: MsgDepositDeploymentResponse
messages.description: MsgDepositDeploymentResponse defines response type for the MsgDepositDeployment.

messages.name: ResourceUnit
messages.description: ResourceUnit extends Resources and adds Count along with the Price.

fields.name: resource
fields.description: Resource holds the amount of resources.

fields.name: count
fields.description: Count corresponds to the amount of replicas to run of the resources.

fields.name: price
fields.description: Price holds the pricing for the resource units.

messages.name: GroupSpec
messages.description:
GroupSpec defines a specification for a group in a deployment on the network.
This includes attributes like the group name, placement requirements, and resource units.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Name is the name of the group.

fields.name: requirements
fields.description:
Requirements specifies the placement requirements for the group.
This determines where the resources in the group can be deployed.

fields.name: resources
fields.description:
Resources is a list containing the resource units allocated to the group.
Each ResourceUnit defines the specific resources (e.g., CPU, memory) assigned.
fields.label: repeated

messages.name: MsgCloseDeployment
messages.description: MsgCloseDeployment defines an SDK message for closing deployment

fields.name: id
fields.description: ID is the unique identifier of the deployment.

messages.name: MsgCloseDeploymentResponse
messages.description: MsgCloseDeploymentResponse defines the Msg/CloseDeployment response type.

messages.name: MsgCreateDeployment
messages.description: MsgCreateDeployment defines an SDK message for creating deployment.

fields.name: id
fields.description: ID is the unique identifier of the deployment.

fields.name: groups
fields.description:
GroupSpec is a list of group specifications for the deployment.
This field is required and must be a list of GroupSpec.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: hash
fields.description: Hash of the deployment.

fields.name: deposit
fields.description: Deposit specifies the amount of coins to include in the deployment's first deposit.

fields.name: depositor
fields.description:
Depositor is the account address of the user who will deposit funds to the deployment.
This value can be different than the owner of the deployment if there is authorized spend grants applied.
It is a string representing a valid account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

messages.name: MsgCreateDeploymentResponse
messages.description: MsgCreateDeploymentResponse defines the Msg/CreateDeployment response type.

messages.name: MsgUpdateDeployment

fields.name: id
fields.description: ID is the unique identifier of the deployment.

fields.name: hash
fields.description: Hash of the deployment.

messages.name: MsgUpdateDeploymentResponse
messages.description: MsgUpdateDeploymentResponse defines the Msg/UpdateDeployment response type.

messages.name: DeploymentFilters
messages.description: DeploymentFilters defines filters used to filter deployments.

fields.name: owner
fields.description:
Owner is the account bech32 address of the user who owns the deployment.
It is a string representing a valid bech32 account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

fields.name: dseq
fields.description:
Dseq (deployment sequence number) is a unique numeric identifier for the deployment.
It is used to differentiate deployments created by the same owner.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State defines the sate of the deployment. A deployment can be either active or inactive.

messages.name: GroupFilters
messages.description: GroupFilters defines filters used to filter groups

fields.name: owner

Owner is the account address of the user who owns the group.
It is a string representing a valid account address.

fields.name: dseq

Dseq (deployment sequence number) is a unique numeric identifier for the deployment.
It is used to differentiate deployments created by the same owner.

fields.name: gseq

Gseq (group sequence number) is a unique numeric identifier for the group.
It is used to differentiate groups created by the same owner in a deployment.

enums.name: State
enums.description: State is an enum which refers to state of group.

values.name: invalid

messages.name: Group

fields.name: state

fields.name: group_spec
fields.description: GroupSpec holds the specification of a the Group.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: CreatedAt is the block height at which the deployment was created.

MsgUpdateParams is the Msg/UpdateParams request type.

fields.name: authority

fields.name: params

Params defines the x/deployment parameters to update.

NOTE: All parameters must be supplied.

values.name: closed
values.description: AccountClosed is the state when an account is closed.

values.name: overdrawn
values.description: AccountOverdrawn is the state when an account is overdrawn.

messages.name: Account
messages.description: Account stores state for an escrow account.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Id is the unique identifier for an escrow account.

fields.name: owner
fields.description:
Owner is the bech32 address of the account.
It is a string representing a valid account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

fields.name: state
fields.description: State represents the current state of an Account.

fields.name: balance
fields.description: Balance holds the unspent coins received from the owner's wallet.

fields.name: transferred
fields.description: Transferred total coins spent by this account.

fields.name: settled_at
fields.description: SettledAt represents the block height at which this account was last settled.

fields.name: depositor
fields.description:
Depositor is the bech32 address of the depositor.
It is a string representing a valid account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."
If depositor is same as the owner, then any incoming coins are added to the Balance.
If depositor isn't same as the owner, then any incoming coins are added to the Funds.

fields.name: funds
fields.description:
Funds are unspent coins received from the (non-Owner) Depositor's wallet.
If there are any funds, they should be spent before spending the Balance.

messages.name: QueryDeploymentRequest
messages.description: QueryDeploymentRequest is request type for the Query/Deployment RPC method.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Id is the unique identifier of the deployment.

messages.name: QueryDeploymentResponse
messages.description: QueryDeploymentResponse is response type for the Query/Deployment RPC method

fields.name: deployment
fields.description: Deployment represents a deployment on the network.

fields.name: groups
fields.description: Groups is a list of deployment groups.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: escrow_account
fields.description:
EscrowAccount represents an escrow mechanism where funds are held.
This ensures that obligations of both tenants and providers involved in the
transaction are met without direct access to each other's accounts.

methods.name: StartGroup
methods.description: StartGroup defines a method to close a group of a deployment given proper inputs.

methods.name: UpdateParams
methods.description:
UpdateParams defines a governance operation for updating the x/deployment module
parameters. The authority is hard-coded to the x/gov module account.

fields.description: DepositParams defines all the parameters related to deposits.

messages.name: Params
messages.description: Params defines the parameters for the x/deployment package.

fields.name: inflation_decay_factor
fields.description: InflationDecayFactor is the number of years it takes inflation to halve.

fields.name: initial_inflation
fields.description:
InitialInflation is the rate at which inflation starts at genesis.
It is a decimal value in the range [0.0, 100.0].

fields.name: variance
fields.description:
Variance defines the fraction by which inflation can vary from ideal inflation in a block.
It is a decimal value in the range [0.0, 1.0].

messages.name: BidID
messages.description:
BidID stores owner and all other seq numbers.
A successful bid becomes a Lease(ID).

fields.name: owner
fields.description:
Owner is the account bech32 address of the user who owns the deployment.
It is a string representing a valid bech32 account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

fields.name: dseq
fields.description:
Dseq (deployment sequence number) is a unique numeric identifier for the deployment.
It is used to differentiate deployments created by the same owner.

fields.name: gseq
fields.description:
Gseq (group sequence number) is a unique numeric identifier for the group.
It is used to differentiate groups created by the same owner in a deployment.

fields.name: oseq
fields.description:
Oseq (order sequence) distinguishes multiple orders associated with a single deployment.
Oseq is incremented when a lease associated with an existing deployment is closed, and a new order is generated.

fields.name: provider
fields.description:
Provider is the account bech32 address of the provider making the bid.
It is a string representing a valid account bech32 address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

messages.name: OrderID
messages.description: OrderId stores owner and all other seq numbers.

values.name: closed

messages.name: Lease
messages.description:
Lease stores LeaseID, state of lease and price.
The Lease defines the terms under which the provider allocates resources to fulfill
the tenant's deployment requirements.
Leases are paid from the tenant to the provider through a deposit and withdraw mechanism and are priced in blocks.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Id is the unique identifier of the Lease.

fields.name: state
fields.description: State represents the state of the Lease.

fields.name: price
fields.description: Price holds the settled price for the Lease.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: CreatedAt is the block height at which the Lease was created.

fields.name: closed_on
fields.description: ClosedOn is the block height at which the Lease was closed.

messages.name: LeaseID
messages.description: LeaseID stores bid details of lease.

fields.name: owner
fields.description:
Owner is the account bech32 address of the user who owns the deployment.
It is a string representing a valid bech32 account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

fields.name: dseq
fields.description:
Dseq (deployment sequence number) is a unique numeric identifier for the deployment.
It is used to differentiate deployments created by the same owner.

fields.name: gseq
fields.description:
Gseq (group sequence number) is a unique numeric identifier for the group.
It is used to differentiate groups created by the same owner in a deployment.

fields.name: oseq
fields.description:
Oseq (order sequence) distinguishes multiple orders associated with a single deployment.
Oseq is incremented when a lease associated with an existing deployment is closed, and a new order is generated.

fields.name: provider
fields.description:
Provider is the account bech32 address of the provider making the bid.
It is a string representing a valid account bech32 address.

Example:

messages.name: EventProviderCreated
messages.description:
EventProviderCreated defines an SDK message for provider created event.
It contains all the required information to identify a provider on-chain.

fields.name: owner
fields.description:
Owner is the bech32 address of the account of the provider.
It is a string representing a valid account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

messages.name: Info

fields.name: email
fields.description: Email is the email address to contact the provider.

fields.name: website
fields.description: Website is the URL to the landing page or socials of the provider.

messages.name: Provider
messages.description:
Provider stores owner and host details.
Akash providers are entities that contribute computing resources to the network.
They can be individuals or organizations with underutilized computing resources, such as data centers or personal servers.
Providers participate in the network by running the Akash node software and setting the price for their services.
Users can then choose a provider based on factors such as cost, performance, and location.

fields.name: owner
fields.description:
Owner is the bech32 address of the account of the provider.
It is a string representing a valid account address.

Example:
  "akash1..."

fields.name: host_uri
fields.description:
HostURI is the Uniform Resource Identifier for provider connection.
This URI is used to directly connect to the provider to perform tasks such as sending the manifest.

fields.name: attributes
fields.description: Attributes is a list of arbitrary attribute key-value pairs.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: info
fields.description: Info contains additional provider information.

messages.name: GenesisState
messages.description: GenesisState defines the basic genesis state used by provider module.

fields.name: providers
fields.description: Providers is a list of genesis providers.
fields.label: repeated

scalarValueTypes.notes: Uses variable-length encoding. Inefficient for encoding negative numbers – if your field is likely to have negative values, use sint32 instead.
Uses variable-length encoding. Inefficient for encoding negative numbers – if your field is likely to have negative values, use sint64 instead.
Uses variable-length encoding. Signed int value. These more efficiently encode negative numbers than regular int32s.
Uses variable-length encoding. Signed int value. These more efficiently encode negative numbers than regular int64s.
Always four bytes. More efficient than uint32 if values are often greater than 2^28.
Always eight bytes. More efficient than uint64 if values are often greater than 2^56.
A string must always contain UTF-8 encoded or 7-bit ASCII text.
May contain any arbitrary sequence of bytes.
